title: Who do I become as the start of the new line?
subtitle: A journal set for writing yourself into the role of the family's turning point.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for exploring what it means to be the person who starts a new money legacy in your family. Five prompts, thirty minutes, drawn from Day 13 of Rebecca's MONEY program.
sourceNotes: Drawn from Day 13 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and Week 2 of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use after completing the Day 13 tapping or energy statement, when you want to write into what the new lineage actually looks like.

content.text: A journal set for Day 13 of the MONEY program: the week when the work shifts from releasing what was inherited to deciding what comes next. Five prompts, around thirty minutes.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
What has money meant in my family until now?
Write for five minutes without editing. Name the patterns, the sayings, the silences.
What does the idea of being the turning point bring up?
Excitement, fear, doubt — write what is actually present, not what you think should be there.
Who do I become as the start of the new line?
Describe the woman who holds this role. How she handles money. What she says to her children.
What am I already doing that she would do?
Name the concrete things, however small. This is evidence, not aspiration.
What is the first thing that changes?
One specific shift — in habit, belief, or action. Not the whole picture: the first thing.
Drawn from Day 13 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and Week 2 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
